title: Seeking intellectual property advice
body: "Get free advice from information centres near you  You can access guidance and support through a network of 25 PatLib Centres (Patent Libraries) covering all regions of the UK. The services usually available include:    free 1 hour business and IP 1:1 (appointment only) workshops and seminars referrals to local IP attorneys   The British Library Business & IP Centre across the UK supports entrepreneurs, inventors and small businesses. They provide help from initial inspiration to successfully launching and growing a business.  Book a training course  Learn from the Intellectual Property Office experts who register and grant trade marks, designs and patents.  There are a variety of Intellectual Property Training courses  available. These are suitable for business owners, inventors or anyone with an interest in IP.  Attend an event to speak to our team  Our team of advisors travel across the UK and provide guidance at events which have an intellectual property or business focus.  You may need to register for the event to attend, but our team provide free seminars, workshops and 1:1 guidance on how intellectual property can help businesses.  Business advisors and advisory resources  A list of organisations who offer business and IP advice to innovators and businesses.  This is not an exhaustive list but is a good starting point for businesses looking for intellectual property and general business advice.  Customer Support Centre  Our Customer Support Centre is open Monday to Friday, 9am until 5pm. Our team of advisors will be able to provide support and answer any questions you have.  You can contact us on 0300 300 2000 or by email information@ipo.gov.uk  Interactive resources  Watch videos on YouTube to learn about:   is intellectual property important to my business? should I get a trade mark? should I get a patent? should I have a registered design? is copyright important to my business?   You can also access free and easy to use IPO online support tools that can help you identify your IP assets and provide you with the next steps on how to protect them.  Get legal advice  We strongly recommend you seek legal advice. You should read our Get legal advice from an intellectual property professional before applying for intellectual property.  "
